what_it_does: This file is a YAML-frontmatter metadata record for a channel-memory attachment; it declares an attachment ID, channel, attachment type, and a target board item URI with an added timestamp, but contains no further body content.
observations: The file consists solely of YAML frontmatter between triple dashes.
Fields include id (att-87860505), channel (dragnet-development), attachment_type (board_items), target (board://item-431e990f), and added (2026-06-24T01:32:19.782650+00:00).
The markdown body after the frontmatter is empty.
The target uses a board:// URI scheme referencing item-431e990f.
standouts: The attachment target is a board:// URI rather than a file path or web URL.
The file is a minimal metadata stub with no explanatory text or payload.
It links a channel-memory attachment to a board item in the dragnet-development channel.
conventions: Follows a YAML-frontmatter schema with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
Uses an att-{uuid}.md naming convention for attachment files.
References external artifacts via a custom board:// URI scheme.
concerns: No body content or context is provided beyond the metadata fields.
The board:// target cannot be resolved or verified from within this file alone.
There is no provenance field such as author or editor.
notes: A bare attachment metadata stub that records a link from dragnet-development channel memory to board item item-431e990f.
questions: What content or object does board://item-431e990f represent?
What does attachment_type 'board_items' imply about expected behavior or rendering?
Was any payload or body content intended to accompany this metadata?
Who created or last modified this attachment?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears intended to serve as a channel-memory attachment record; it does exactly that, but only as a metadata stub with no additional payload.
apparent_themes: Traceability of channel artifacts to board items
intention_signals: It seems designed to create a discoverable, timestamped association between a channel-memory attachment identifier and a specific board item so the attachment can be retrieved by ID or by its target reference.
novelty: The use of a board:// URI scheme as the target value and the extreme minimalism of the record (only frontmatter, no body) are distinctive.
contribution: It contributes a persistent, timestamped association between attachment att-87860505 and board item item-431e990f within the dragnet-development channel, which a fused system could retain as a linkage record.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment record named att-87860505 in the dragnet-development channel, typed board_items, pointing to board://item-431e990f and added on 2026-06-24T01:32:19.782650+00:00, with no body content.
intention_for_embedding: To record and make retrievable a link from a channel-memory attachment to a specific board item in the dragnet-development channel.
